trails.name: NICHOLSONS TRACK
trails.description: This short track takes you from the top of Symes Road through beautiful tussock country to Nicholson Hut.

trails.name: OBELISK LOOP TRACK
trails.description: This loop starts from the highest point on the range at Kopuwai. On a good day, you can see as far out as Rakiura/Stewart Island, Aoraki/Mt Cook, and Mt Aspiring/Tititea.

trails.name: PROSPECT HILL TRACK
trails.description: From this track on a clear day you can see the Manuherikia basin, the Old Woman, Dunstan and Raggedy ranges.

trails.name: THE SISTERS TRACK
trails.description: Tramp through herb fields and snow tussock while enjoying great views of the Alexandra basin, Old Woman Range and The Remarkables Mountain range.

trails.name: LOOP TRACK CONROYS CREEK HISTORIC SITES
BUTCHERS DAM LOOP TRACK
trails.description: Butchers Dam Loop Track is an excellent short family walk.

trails.name: OMEO GULLY TRACK
trails.description: Omeo Gully Track goes through one of Otago’s highest, most isolated gold fields. View distinctive gold workings on high sluice faces, deeply riveted tail races, tall stacked tailings and the remains of dwellings.

trails.name: LONG GULLY LOOP TRACK
trails.description: Weave your way through dramatic rock bluffs to the Long Gully Bluffs Conservation Area to get fantastic views of Lake Dunstan and the St Bathans Range - it's a steep but rewarding climb.

trails.name: Gabriels Gully Interpretation Walk
trails.description: Explore Gabriels Gully and the stories of goldfield occupants with a series of interpretation panels as your guide, and picnic at Greys Dam.

trails.name: Otago Dam Track
trails.description: Follow the track to an old water race and visit the historic Otago Dam.

trails.name: TITAN ROCKS TRACK
trails.description: This marked route offers excellent views from above the bush line.

trails.name: SHEK HARN HISTORIC RESERVE TRACK
trails.description: A walking track along the Fraser River to dwellings used by Chinese miners and the remains of gold workings.

trails.name: EARNSCLEUGH TAILINGS TRACK
trails.description: The tailings on this walk are nationally significant as the only complete record of dredging activity from 1863 to 1963. They're an awe inspiring sight, hundreds of metres wide like a giant’s ploughed field.

trails.name: ROXBURGH FACES TRAMPING TRACK
trails.description: This track connects Flat Top Hill to the Roxburgh Gorge Trail. It is also called Haul Road East.

trails.name: Bannockburn Sluicings
trails.description: This vanished landscape is New Zealand’s own wild west: The Bannockburn Sluicings are the scarred landscapes of a short-lived goldmining era.

trails.name: Lake Roxburgh Walkway
trails.description: This historic trail follows an old bridle path that transported coal to the gold mining dredges 1890s-1930s.

trails.name: Piano Flat & Waikaia Forest tramping tracks
trails.description: Waikaia Forest is a large island of beech forest, nestled in the mountain farmlands of northern Southland. Piano Flat, a grassy enclosure at the southern end, provides a picnic area, camping ground and several walking tracks.

trails.name: Munro’s Gully Track
trails.description: Explore the beech forest and water races right next to the site of New Zealand’s first goldrush and discover historic dams that powered the shifting of entire hills.

trails.name: Long Gully Loop Track
trails.description: Weave your way through dramatic rock bluffs to the Long Gully Bluffs Conservation Area to get fantastic views of Lake Dunstan and the St Bathans Range - it's a steep but rewarding climb.

trails.name: Slapjack Saddle Track
trails.description: Journey through the Carrick Range, past the spectacular Long Gully bluffs. There are brilliant views from the tops and golden colours in the lower reaches during autumn.

trails.name: Carrick Range access tracks
trails.description: Rising above Bannockburn and rich in goldmining history, the Carrick Range is one of the most accessible mountain ranges in Central Otago. These tracks take you to three conservation areas and two historic reserves.

trails.name: Cairnmuir Hill Track
trails.description: An all-day walk from Clyde to Bannockburn over the crest of the Cairnmuir Mountains.

trails.name: Conroys Creek Historic Sites
trails.description: Explore the historic relics and admire the views in the Aldinga Conservation Area. Conroys Dam is a popular picnic and swimming spot.

trails.name: Potters No. 2 Tramping Track, Huts to Grave
trails.description: This is a short tramping track through the protected historic site in the impressive landscape of the Campbell Creek catchment.

trails.name: Obelisk Loop Track Track
trails.description: This loop starts from the highest point on the range at Kopuwai. On a good day, you can see as far out as Rakiura/Stewart Island, Aoraki/Mt Cook, and Mt Aspiring/Tititea.

trails.name: Kopuwai Track to Sisters Track
trails.description: Centred in the heart of the Kopuwai Conservation Area, this track follows along the rolling tops of the Old Man Range.

trails.name: Flat Top Hill Tramping Track
trails.description:
This loop track is an extension of the Basin View Tramping Track. It has a 300 m climb 
with extensive views.
